meaning: n.一、二年级学生,下层阶级
examples: So far, it's helped only the middle class, not the massive underclass, which remains at risk of intimidation and violence.
The basic problems of the inner-city underclass are inadequate housing and lack of jobs.
Part of an Underclass Since graduating in 2004, Kyoko has held six low-paying jobs.
The charity says high rates of divorce and relationship breakdown are contributing to the "hidden underclass".
The French press suggests that Anglo-Saxon capitalism has produced a hopeless white underclass unique in Europe.
